Land Rover Discovery 4.4 V8 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery 4.4 V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 4.4 V8 engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only. Used engines are available from £1,500–£2,800. Fitting adds £1,300–£2,000 depending on your location and workshop. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Discovery.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery 4.4 V8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 4.4 AJ-V8 is widely regarded as one of the most reliable petrol engines ever fitted to a Discovery. It's a smooth, naturally aspirated V8 with no turbocharger complexity — a 'bulletproof' design when properly maintained. However, it has known weak points: plastic timing chain tensioners that can crack over time, cooling system leaks, and VVT phaser wear. Regular maintenance is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Discovery 4.4 V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are timing chain tensioner wear (plastic secondary tensioners crack, causing cold-start rattle), cooling system leaks from the thermostat housing or water pump, and VVT phaser failure. The engine is all-aluminium, so overheating must be avoided.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery 4.4 V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £8,000 Discovery 3, a £4,500 recon is 56% of the car's value — approaching our 60% Land Rover threshold. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon exceeds the threshold — get a scrap valuation first. However, well-maintained 4.4 V8 Discoveries are becoming collectable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery 4.4 V8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 4.4 V8 carries the engine code 448PN (also referred to as AJ-V8 or AJ41 4.4 in some documentation). It's a 4.4-litre naturally aspirated V8 petrol engine used in the Discovery 3 (L319) from 2004 to 2009.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain tensioners, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative secondary timing chain tensioner replacement costs £800–£1,800. If ignored and a tensioner fails completely, the chain can skip, causing valve-to-piston contact and catastrophic engine failure. Aftermarket kits with metal tensioners are available and fully interchangeable with the original plastic units. At the first sign of cold-start rattle lasting more than 3 seconds, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery 4.4 V8 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the AJ-V8 4.4-litre petrol engine. Used 4.4 V8 engines are available from approximately £1,500–£2,800, with reconditioned units ranging from £3,500–£5,500.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery 4.4 V8 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare 4.4 V8 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 3+ seconds, metallic ticking from the engine, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,800 (secondary tensioner replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£6,500 (if chain skips and valves contact pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle lasting more than 3 seconds, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The 4.4 AJ-V8 uses plastic secondary timing chain tensioners that can crack over time. If a tensioner fails completely, the chain can skip, causing valve-to-piston contact and catastrophic engine failure. The good news: aftermarket kits with metal tensioners are available and fully interchangeable with the original plastic units. At the first sign of cold-start rattle lasting more than 3 seconds, get the tensioners inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Cooling System Leaks
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, overheating, sweet smell from the engine bay.
problems.recommendation: Coolant leaks from the thermostat housing or water pump are common on both petrol and diesel Discoveries. The 4.4 V8's all-aluminium construction means overheating can quickly lead to warped heads or head gasket failure. If you're experiencing unexplained coolant loss, have the cooling system inspected promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: VVT Phaser Failure
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, loss of power, poor fuel economy, engine warning light with VVT-related codes.
problems.recommendation: The 4.4 V8's variable valve timing system relies on VVT phasers that can wear over time. If ignored, failed phasers can cause timing issues and eventually contribute to timing chain problems. Regular oil changes using the correct 5W-30 specification are essential — these phasers are hydraulically actuated and sensitive to oil quality and pressure.

failureLink.label: Read the full VVT failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery 4.4 V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic engine failure
Specialist repair available; cheaper than engine replacement
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 4.4 V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing chain tensioner inspection or replacement history
* Cooling system service records (thermostat housing, water pump replaced)
* Full service history with 5W-30 oil changes at 10,000-mile (or less) intervals
* No cold-start rattle — a quiet startup suggests healthy tensioners
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle lasting more than 3 seconds with no timing chain history
* Unexplained coolant loss with no cooling system service records
* Poor service history — this V8 is durable but unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with VVT-related codes and no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 4.4 V8 is one of the most reliable petrol engines ever fitted to a Discovery — a smooth, naturally aspirated V8 with none of the turbo or diesel complexity that plagues other variants. But it's an ageing engine now, and plastic timing chain tensioners and cooling system components are the price of entry. Buy on documented history, not on faith — a well-maintained 4.4 V8 Discovery 3 is a £6,000–£10,000 future classic; a neglected one is a £3,500 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare 4.4 V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery 4.4 V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling lasting more than 3 seconds (timing chain tensioner wear warning)
* Service History — including the 4.4 V8 era (2004–2009) and confirmed oil-change intervals using 5W-30 spec oil
* Coolant Check — Any history of coolant loss or thermostat housing/water pump replacement?
* Oil Pressure — Check the oil pressure warning light; low pressure can indicate timing chain tensioner issues
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate coolant ingress from cooling system failure
* VVT Operation — Any hesitation, rough idle, or loss of power? (VVT phaser wear warning)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, VVT, or cooling system issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (cats, O2 sensors)
